Letters to The Editor — June 19, 2026

2026-06-20.topic: The world’s largest grand piano was built by a 15-year-old in New Zealand. The piano is 18 feet and 9 inches long and has 85 keys, three fewer than the standard 88 keys.
A light year is the distance light travels in one year, measuring about 5.88 trillion miles (9.46 trillion kilometers), and it is a unit of distance, not time.
Unlike most primates, male night monkeys are the primary caregivers and carry their infant almost constantly after the first week, returning it to the mother mainly for nursing.
Some regions of the world consider continents differently. The most widely accepted number is 7, but some places consider there to be as few as 4.
The Divje Babe flute, dated to 50,000-60,000 years ago, may be the world’s oldest musical instrument, though some attribute its holes to animal bites.
Calling the number twenty a “score” dates back to the Old Norse skor (meaning “notch” or “cut”), since large quantities were once tallied by making a notch for every twenty.
Instead of having just one head of state, Switzerland is led by a council of seven who collectively share the role.
Trypanophobia is the extreme fear of needles and is effectively treated with exposure therapy, often supplemented with strategies like topical numbing, distraction, and smaller needles.
In Balkan folklore, anyone born on a Saturday can see vampires when they turn invisible.
There are over 196 different flavors of Pringles. This includes mushroom and cream, pecan pie, and white chocolate peppermint.
Emperor penguins have been recorded diving as deep as 1,850 feet (550 meters) and can hold their breath for more than 20 minutes.
June 20 is World Productivity Day, a day focused on enhancing efficiency and motivation by encouraging individuals to accomplish tasks and manage time effectively.
Top Gear set a Toyota Hilux on fire, submerged it, hit it with a wrecking ball, and buried it in a building collapse; each time it was repaired without spare parts and restarted.
Vanuatu’s national anthem is called “Yumi, yumi, yumi,” meaning “We, we, we” in the country’s creole language, Bislama.
Mel Brooks helped produce the 1986 film “The Fly” but chose to be uncredited so that audiences would not assume it was a comedy.
Astronomers discovered a giant water reservoir floating in space that is equivalent to 140 trillion times all the water in the world’s oceans. And it’s only 12 billion light-years away!
An icosagon is a shape with 20 sides. Both the Big Wheel on “The Price is Right” and the foundations for William Shakespeare’s original Globe Theatre are icosagonal.
Western mathematicians did not accept negative numbers for over 18 centuries. They treated them as absurd solutions to equations.
Tonight, June 20, 2026, the Moon is in its Waxing Crescent phase with 33% illumination, meaning the lit portion we see is still small but increasing each night.
American-Australian actress Nicole Kidman is celebrating her 59th birthday today. She was born on June 20, 1967 in Hawaii, USA, making her zodiac sign Gemini.
While the first half of June was seen as an incredibly unlucky time to get married in ancient Rome, the second half was a favorite!
Scientists found a gene in fruit flies that doubled their average lifespan under certain conditions. They named the gene “Indy,” short for “I’m not dead yet,” a line from Monty Python.
Aardvarks have lengthy, adhesive tongues that can be up to 12 inches (30 cm) long.
Eucla, a small coastal town in Western Australia with fewer than 100 residents, has its own time zone, which is UTC+8:45.
Fruits and vegetables exhibit circadian rhythms, meaning they can distinguish between day and night and may even become jet-lagged when moved across time zones.
Smuckers Uncrustables sends all of the crusts they cut off their sandwiches to be made into animal feed.
A vigintillion is 1 followed by 63 zeros and is so large that Lovecraft used it to describe the incomprehensible passage of time leading to Cthulhu’s release in “The Call of Cthulhu.”
In 2016, American workers forfeited 206 million vacation days, resulting in $66.4 billion in lost benefits.
Bhutan’s constitution mandates that at least 60% of the nation must remain forested. As a result, this small mountain nation is absolutely teeming with wildlife.
In 2017, 70 students drank so much alcohol at a Maryland house party that the air inside the house registered 0.01% on a breathalyzer.

2026-06-21.topic: Research suggests that women tend to be better at distinguishing colors, while men often excel at tracking fast-moving objects and recognizing details from a distance.
In 2008, a Buzz Lightyear toy spent 15 months aboard the International Space Station as part of an educational partnership between NASA and Disney Pixar.
In 2019, a state of emergency was declared in one of Russia’s Arctic regions due to a mass invasion of polar bears.
The historical name of Czechia was “Bohemia.” Boiohaemum, as it was originally known in Latin, comes from the Germanic “Boi-haima,” meaning “home of the Boii.”
The name Alfred means “elf counsel” in Old English, combining “ælf” (elf) and “ræd” (counsel). One of the earliest famous Alfreds was Alfred the Great.
The 21st card in a tarot deck, The World, represents the completion of a journey and the sense of fulfillment that comes with reaching an important milestone.
Studies have found that names can affect first impressions, teacher expectations, or job callbacks, but life success is mostly shaped by family background and opportunity.
Trypophobia is a fear of clustered holes or repetitive circular patterns. Kendall Jenner has this fear and said the sight of such patterns gives her overwhelming anxiety.
Sunday is the second day of the week in the old Persian calendar, used in Afghanistan and Iran. Funnily enough, it’s called day one, as their week begins on day zero, Saturday.
Saparmurat Niyazov, Turkmenistan’s dictator from 1991 to 2006, imposed some bizarre laws, including banning opera, ballet, circuses, and lip-syncing at concerts.
Unlike most snakes, the female king cobra builds a nest from dry leaves and other vegetation before laying her eggs and guarding them during the incubation period.
June 21 is International T-Shirt Day, a celebration of the most common clothing choice worldwide, marked by wearing favorite tees or shopping for new ones.
In 2017, the restaurant chain Chili’s jokingly lifted a fictional 11-year ban on Pam Beesly, who had been banned from the restaurant in a 2005 episode of The Office.
Pakistan is the only Islamic nation in the world to have developed and demonstrated nuclear capability.
Ricky Nelson’s “Poor Little Fool” made history as the very first song to hit number one on Billboard’s Hot 100 on August 4, 1958.
Supernovas happen when certain stars run out of fuel. When this happens, the star collapses in on itself, creating a shockwave that tears it apart.
An icosihenagon is a shape with 21 sides. It’s often used in advanced math problems that involve tessellation and by artists and architects to break symmetry in circular patterns.
Off-Broadway is defined by seating capacity, which ranges from 100 to 499, and does not refer to a specific location but to any theater venue in New York City.
Tonight, June 21, 2026, the Moon is in its Waxing Crescent phase with 43% illumination, meaning the lit portion we see is still small but increasing each night.
American singerLana Del Reyis celebrating her 41st birthday today. She was born on June 21, 1985 in New York, USA, making her zodiac sign Cancer.
While the first half of June was seen as an incredibly unlucky time to get married in ancient Rome, the second half was a favorite!
In 1866, Alfred Nobel originally planned to name his invention “Nobel’s Safety Powder” before deciding on the name dynamite.
Capybaras eat their own feces to fully digest the hard grass that predominantly makes up their diet.
Will’s signature bowl cut in Stranger Things was originally inspired by Bastian’s hairstyle in the 1984 fantasy film The NeverEnding Story.
Research found that Guinness drinkers with mustaches lose about 0.02 ounces, or 0.6 milliliters, of beer each time they take a sip, as foam becomes trapped in their facial hair.
The human eye can see as many as 10 million different shades of color, all of which are interpreted by receptors that distinguish between red, green, and blue light waves.
The first color a baby can see is red, which they usually see within a few weeks of birth.
Psychologists at UC Berkeley found that pressure to feel upbeat can make you feel downbeat. Embracing your darker moods can make you feel better in the long run.
In Japan, families clean and decorate their homes on New Year’s Eve to welcome Toshigami, the New Year’s god.
A priest and book publisher devised recipes inspired by biblical foods, including a locust soup to represent John the Baptist’s diet of “locusts and wild honey.”

2026-06-22.topic: Antimatter is the most expensive substance on Earth, costing roughly $62.5 trillion per gram, or $1.75 quadrillion per ounce.
Mercury and Venus are the only two planets in the solar system that do not have any moons.
The kookaburra is native to Australia and New Guinea; its calls are often used as sound effects in movies for jungles in Africa or South America.
Although there are millions of islands on Earth, only around 11,000 are permanently inhabited by humans.
The odds of giving birth to identical twins is 1 in every 250 pregnancies.
The number 22 wasn’t meant to feature in Joseph Heller’s “Catch-22,” the book or the paradox. It was meant to be “Catch-18,” but a popular book called “Mila 18” had just been published.
In 1797, William Charles Cole Claiborne was elected to the U.S. Congress at 22, despite the minimum age being 25, due to his exceptional qualifications and popularity.
Vehophobia is the intense fear of driving, often developing after car accidents. Studies show that 42-77% of people involved in serious crashes later experience driving-related anxiety.
Monday is the only day of the week with a valid English anagram, where its letters can be rearranged to spell the word “dynamo.”
Los Pinos, once the residence of Mexico’s presidents, is 14 times the size of the White House and became accessible to the public on December 1, 2018.
A hamster’s cheek pouches stretch back to its shoulders, allowing it to carry food nearly equal to its own body weight in a single trip.
June 22 is World Rainforest Day, a day dedicated to raising awareness about the challenges faced by rainforests and encouraging actions to protect these vital ecosystems.
Baikonur Cosmodrome in Kazakhstan is the world’s largest fully operational launch site, used for both crewed and uncrewed rockets, and leased to Russia.
On March 6, 1957, Ghana became the first sub-Saharan African nation to gain independence from colonial rule.
American bacon usually comes from pork belly, while British back bacon comes from the loin with some belly attached, which makes it leaner than American streaky bacon.
NASA scientists reported in 2015 that Mars once held more water than Earth’s Arctic Ocean, forming a vast ocean that covered up to 19% of the planet’s surface.
An icosidigon is a shape with 22 sides, and it’s primarily used in geometry and educational software to explore properties of high-sided polygons. Drawn to scale, it’s nearly circular.
Certain hospitals in Rwanda can order blood for transfusions and have it delivered via drone.
Tonight, June 22, 2026, the Moon is in its First Quarter phase, meaning it is one quarter of the way through its orbit around Earth.
American actress Meryl Streep is celebrating her 77th birthday today. She was born on June 22, 1949 in New Jersey, USA, making her zodiac sign Cancer.
While the first half of June was seen as an incredibly unlucky time to get married in ancient Rome, the second half was a favorite!
One of South Korea’s worst oil spills occurred on December 7, 2007, and within 33 days, more than one million civilians, soldiers, and officials had volunteered to help with the cleanup.
Badgers obtain the majority of their food by digging underground, with worms making up about 60–80% of a European badger’s diet.
USB stands for Universal Serial Bus. “Universal” means it works with many kinds of devices, “serial” means data is sent in sequence, and “bus” means a shared connection system.
“Daughter from California” syndrome is a phrase used in medicine to describe long-lost relatives who suddenly appear and insist a dying relative’s life be prolonged.
The big toe is scientifically known as the hallux. Unlike the other four toes, the hallux typically has only two bones instead of three.
Frank Sinatra famously said that “orange is the happiest color,” and that wearing it filled him with confidence and a sense of joy.
When coffee first emerged in the Ottoman Empire in the 16th century, it was considered a drug, and its consumption was forbidden.
In 2010, Abu Dhabi’s Emirates Palace Hotel displayed a $10,000 artificial tree decorated with $11 million worth of jewels, including diamonds and emeralds.
Historically, an “outlaw” was someone who was no longer protected by the law as punishment for a crime, allowing members of the public to persecute them at will legally.
